system_description: Vietnamese isolating disambiguation. Analytic language with zero inflection: no case, no agreement, no tense morphology. Disambiguation relies on strict SVO word order and a system of aspect/mood particles (da = perfective, dang = progressive, se = future, roi = completion). Classifiers (cai, con, nguoi, etc.) mark noun categories but carry semantic rather than grammatical information. Closest to Mandarin in the typological space but with an additional complication: tonal distinctions (6 tones) carry lexical information that word order alone cannot recover. Written form loses tonal context.
notes: PRE-REGISTERED prediction: k_eff ~2.9-4.1, similar to or slightly above Mandarin. Vietnamese is isolating like Mandarin but with fewer discourse-structure markers. Both word_order and aspect_particles fire late in the parse (clause-level). The pre-registration predicts Vietnamese k_eff >= Mandarin k_eff. Falsifier: if Vietnamese k_eff < Mandarin k_eff, something about Vietnamese aspect particles provides earlier disambiguation.
